mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 6.5 percent signals a deteriorating labor market that triggers recession expectations, causing institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5 percent, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 0.5 percent month-over-month during an economic expansion confirms robust manufacturing output, which supports short-term equity momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of expansion regime observations, or the out-of-sample hit rate drops below 52 percent over a rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index surpassing 235.0 triggers aggressive central bank tightening expectations, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 70 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0, or the signal exhibits a positive Rank IC over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll employment growth and stable unemployment rates demonstrate effective labor market absorption without slack, which sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of observations, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a pooled cross-sectional regression.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly real GDP growth directly translates to higher aggregate corporate profits, which increases dividend payouts and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDP growth signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 58 percent of quarters, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates that higher interest rates are successfully suppressing output, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of tightening regime observations when INDPRO declines, or the signal shows a positive predictive accuracy over a 2-year out-of-sample window.
